methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Exodys Energy is a Seed-stage nuclear energy company founded by Carl Perez and Jean-Christophe Piroux, having raised $10M+. The nuclear energy sector includes 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers, with notable private companies including Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Fuse Energy, and Applied Atomics.

Stage-progression analysis, the only applicable triangulation method, anchors to the $10M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a standard 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for Seed-to-Series A advancement, implying a valuation of $25M. Peer-median analysis was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage nuclear comparables meeting minimum sample requirements (0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier peers identified). A sector-wide median would introduce unacceptable noise given the dispersed valuation profiles across the nuclear landscape.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, centered at $25M. The narrow range reflects reliance on a single method; key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to the broader nuclear cohort, technology differentiation versus the identified peer set, and the timing and terms of anticipated Series A fundraising. Additional comparables or disclosed metrics on burn rate and technical milestones would strengthen conviction in this estimate.
